description: An Insight record: the computed performance scorecard for one supplier in one period (month/quarter). Built by the two-stage analytics engine: stage 1 normalizes each raw metric to 0-100 against configurable bands; stage 2 weights the four scored dimensions (delivery/quality/cost/responsiveness, default 40/30/20/10) into a composite 0-100, then assigns a grade. Compliance is a HARD eligibility gate, not a weighted input (axiom A15). Carries the distinctly-labelled two-factor OTIF and four-factor perfect-order metrics (axiom A14). The scorecard drives the AVL status that gates PO eligibility and feeds the CAPA/suspend loop.

fields.name: scorecardId
fields.label: Scorecard No.
fields.validation: system-generated; unique; immutable; one per (supplierId, period)
fields.purpose: Stable key for one supplier-period scorecard so the time-series (rolling average, trend slope, streak counters) and the AVL gate reference a specific period's result.

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.validation: an existing supplier; the scorecard's subject; resolved by the normalizer (supplier -> name)
fields.purpose: The supplier being scored; ties the period result to the supplier whose AVL status it gates and whose re-qualification the four-factor perfect-order metric feeds.

fields.name: period
fields.label: Period
fields.validation: the period label e.g. 2026-05 or 2026-Q2; unique per supplier; set at period close
fields.purpose: The month/quarter the scorecard covers; the time bucket the period-over-period trend and the consecutive-at-or-above / consecutive-below streak counters are computed across.

fields.name: qualityScore
fields.label: Quality Score
fields.validation: 0-100; stage-1 normalized from defect rate / PPM / NCR rate against configurable bands
fields.purpose: The 0-100 quality sub-score (defect/PPM/NCR) the composite weights at default 30%; the dimension pharma/food raises. One of the four scored inputs to the weighted roll-up.

fields.name: deliveryScore
fields.label: Delivery Score
fields.validation: 0-100; stage-1 normalized from OTD / fill-rate / OTIF against configurable bands (>=95 green / 90-94 amber / <90 red)
fields.purpose: The 0-100 delivery sub-score (OTD/fill-rate/OTIF) the composite weights at default 40% (the highest default weight); the primary operational performance input.

fields.name: costScore
fields.label: Cost Score
fields.validation: 0-100; stage-1 normalized from PPV / PPV% / TCO against configurable bands (negative PPV favorable; baseline = last purchase price)
fields.purpose: The 0-100 cost sub-score (PPV/TCO vs last-purchase-price baseline) the composite weights at default 20%; the price-competitiveness input to the roll-up.

fields.name: responsivenessScore
fields.label: Responsiveness Score
fields.validation: 0-100; stage-1 normalized from RFQ turnaround / CAPA responsiveness against configurable bands
fields.purpose: The 0-100 service sub-score (RFQ turnaround, CAPA responsiveness) the composite weights at default 10%; the smallest default weight, the supplier-engagement input.

fields.name: weights
fields.label: Dimension Weights
fields.validation: map of dimension -> weight; must sum to 100; configurable per tenant (pharma/food raises quality)
fields.purpose: The configurable weights the composite roll-up applies; stored on the scorecard so the composite is reproducible even after a buyer changes the tenant weights later. Default 40/30/20/10, must sum to 100.

fields.name: composite
fields.label: Composite Score
fields.validation: 0-100; sum(sub-score x weight) over the four scored dimensions
fields.purpose: The single 0-100 weighted performance figure; drives the grade band and, with the compliance gate, the AVL eligibility. The one number that summarizes the supplier's period performance.

fields.name: grade
fields.label: Grade
fields.validation: from composite: >=90 A, 70-89 B, <70 C (ScorecardGrade enum); sustained A across periods marks Preferred
fields.purpose: The letter band derived from composite (A/B/C); the human-readable performance tier shown on dashboards and the input to the Preferred determination (sustained Grade A).

fields.name: complianceGate
fields.label: Compliance Gate Passed
fields.validation: true iff cert validity == 100% AND document completeness passes; a HARD gate, never a weighted input (axiom A15)
fields.purpose: The hard eligibility gate (certs 100% + docs complete) that caps AVL status at Conditional/Suspended on FAIL regardless of a high composite (axiom A15); kept out of the weighted score so a compliant-but-poor and a non-compliant-but-good supplier are never conflated.

fields.name: avlStatus
fields.label: AVL Status
fields.validation: AVL eligible iff complianceGate PASS AND composite >= AVL threshold (default 70); Preferred = sustained Grade A + no red dimension + low risk + competitive TCO; gate FAIL caps at Conditional/Suspended
fields.purpose: The approved-vendor-list standing that gates PO eligibility (sourcing diagram 04 reads it) and feeds the suspend loop; the decision the whole scorecard exists to produce. Suspended blocks new POs (axiom A11).

fields.name: otifTwoFactor
fields.label: OTIF (Two-Factor)
fields.validation: 0-100 %; on-time AND in-full per order line (all-or-nothing intersection); operational streak metric (RA e07)
fields.purpose: The two-factor operational OTIF (on-time AND in-full) the dashboard streak uses; labelled distinctly from the four-factor metric (axiom A14) because on-time-but-short or complete-but-late both fail it.

fields.name: perfectOrderFourFactor
fields.label: Perfect Order (Four-Factor)
fields.validation: {onTime, inFull, damageFree, documentationAccurate, perfectOrderPercent}; on-time AND in-full AND damage-free AND documentation-accurate (SCOR RL.1.2); the re-qualification gate metric
fields.computedFormula: all four factors true / total lines * 100, with factor breakdown
fields.purpose: The four-factor perfect order (the two OTIF factors plus damage-free and documentation-accurate) used by the supplier re-qualification gate; stored as a breakdown so the failing factor is visible, and labelled distinctly from two-factor OTIF (axiom A14, never conflated).

invariants: A14 OTIF computed two ways and labelled distinctly: two-factor (operational streak) vs four-factor perfect order (re-qualification gate); never conflated
A15 compliance (cert validity, document completeness) is a HARD eligibility gate, not a weighted scorecard input
A11 a Suspended AVL status blocks new POs; quality events drive AVL via the CAPA loop
A12 cost metrics use base-currency amounts
A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
two-stage scoring: normalize to 0-100 then weight to a composite; weights sum to 100 (default 40/30/20/10); grade A>=90/B>=70/C<70; AVL = gate PASS AND composite >= threshold (default 70)
